cover.title: THE CANDLE
cover.subtitle: The one who loves through small actions, long before the words come out.
cover.tagline: You loved them deeply. The hard part was letting them see it.

profile_scores.caption: High Care, Low Expression. You love deeply through action, while the words often arrive much later.

short_summary: You're not someone who makes a big show of love.
Once you give your heart to someone, you go in deep. Very deep.
You loved them through everything you did. They just didn't always know what it meant.

full_report.headline: THE CANDLE
full_report.intro: You're not someone who makes a big show of love. You remember their favorite coffee, notice the hard days, and take care of small things without making a speech about it. That isn't indifference. It's one of the clearest ways you love. The problem is that actions can be easy to miss when the words never arrive.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're not someone who makes a big show of love.
Rather than saying "I like you" a hundred times, you're more likely to remember their favorite coffee, their hard days, and the small things that make life easier.
So at first, people can get the wrong idea. They may think you're less invested than you really are.
But your feelings aren't small.
Once you give your heart to someone, you go in deep. Very deep.
You just tend to show it through what you do.
That's why this can hurt so much.
You loved them clearly in your own language.
They just didn't always understand what they were seeing.
chapters.profile_note: High Care, Low Expression. You love deeply through action, while the words often arrive much later.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Keep Repeating: THE SILENT BUILDUP CURVE
body: Early in a relationship, there usually isn't much of a problem.
You're steady. You don't react to every small thing. You give people room.
The trouble starts when something really does hurt.
You don't say it right away. You tell yourself it might be nothing. You try to understand their side one more time.
So you let it go.
Once. Then again. Then again.
But the feeling doesn't disappear. It waits.
And when it finally comes out, the other person thinks it came from nowhere.
For you, it started months ago. The words just arrived today.
chapters.highlight: You're not struggling because you feel too much. You're struggling because you've been carrying it alone for too long.

chart.title: THE SILENT BUILDUP CURVE
chart.description: The hurt keeps building while the words keep arriving late.

chapters.title: What You've Gotten Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: You do get angry. It just takes you longer to show it. By the time the other person notices, you've often been carrying the feeling for a while.
items.truth_short: You don't avoid anger. You often express it late.

items.belief: I thought you'd know without me saying it.
items.truth: That hope makes sense when you notice so much without being told. But people don't always read care, hurt, or disappointment the same way you do. If it matters, give them the words too.
items.truth_short: What feels obvious to you may still need to be said.

items.belief: Holding it in is the mature thing to do.
items.truth: Sometimes waiting helps you calm down. But silence doesn't solve the problem by itself. If the feeling keeps returning, it deserves a conversation before it turns into distance.
items.truth_short: Pausing can help. Carrying it alone for months usually doesn't.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You're often drawn to people who don't show much emotion.
At first, they seem calm. They feel steady. You enjoy discovering them little by little.
But after a while, you can end up doing all the reading.
A shorter reply. A different tone. A pause that lasts a little too long.
You fill in the blanks because you're good at noticing them.
The problem is that they can start expecting the same from you, even though you've said almost nothing.
Love gets tiring when both people are waiting to be understood without speaking.
chapters.highlight: Someone who cares about you notices your actions. Someone who knows you well still needs your words.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need isn't to understand them more. You've already got that covered.
What you need is to say it a little sooner.
When you're hurt. When something feels off. When you expected something different.
You don't need a big speech.
Try one simple sentence on the day it happens.
"That bothered me."
"I wish you'd told me sooner."
"I need a little time, but I do want to talk about this."
The sooner the words arrive, the less the relationship has to guess.
chapters.action_sentence: The sooner the words arrive, the less the relationship has to guess.

chapters.title: Where You Go From Here
body: You already know how to love deeply. You don't give your heart halfway.
That's also why the hurt can stay with you for so long.
What you're learning next is how to stop hiding the important parts.
Saying you like someone when you like them.
Saying you're hurt before the feeling turns into distance.
Saying what you hoped for instead of waiting for them to work it out alone.
You don't need to become louder. You just need your words to arrive while the moment is still alive.
chapters.transition_note: You don't need to become louder. You just need your words to arrive sooner.

chapters.title: The Types You Keep Falling For

items.description: At first, it feels peaceful. You enjoy discovering them slowly. But after a while, every unanswered question can become something you answer alone in your own head.
They always say they're fine. You keep trying to read the rest. A relationship with very little expression can create more guessing than safety.

items.type_title: Someone Who Gets Used to Your Care
items.description: They may not mean harm. They simply start treating your small acts of love like part of the background. When care becomes invisible, affection can slowly turn into exhaustion.

items.type_title: Someone Who Was Sweet at First
items.description: The beginning is full of warmth and attention. Then everyday life arrives. What matters is whether the care stays present after the excitement settles.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: These signs were there early. You just kept finding reasons to let them pass.

signal_strength.label: You let it go even when something upsets you
You explain their behavior on their behalf
There's more and more you want to say, and you don't say any of it
It takes longer and longer to sort through your feelings just to keep the relationship going

chapters.core_signal: They think nothing is wrong. You've already been carrying the same hurt more than once.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like for You

checklist.title: You don't have to guess everything.
checklist.description: Both of you say what you mean instead of leaving the other person to fill in every blank.

checklist.title: It's safe to speak before the feeling gets big.
checklist.description: You can say something hurt without the whole relationship falling apart.

checklist.title: They notice, and you explain.
checklist.description: Attention matters. So do words. The two work best together.

checklist.title: Repair happens before distance takes over.
checklist.description: You don't have to carry the problem alone until the relationship feels completely different.

chapters.title: What Happens In Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: A hard match for you is someone who misses emotional cues and waits for everything to be said perfectly. Because you already speak late, the two of you can leave too much unspoken for too long.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier partner notices small changes, but they don't expect mind reading from either of you. They ask, listen, and make it easier for you to say what happened before the feeling gets buried.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to calm people who don't express much. That doesn't decide how the relationship will go. What matters is whether both of you learn to speak before silence turns into distance.
sections.next_signs: The signs may show up in small moments. A reply feels colder. A plan changes without warning. A tone stays with you. Instead of deciding what it means alone, notice whether the two of you can talk about it while it's still small.
sections.try_this: Choose one small thing that bothered you and say it plainly. Not as a test. Not as a final verdict. Just give them the real information. Then pay attention to what happens next. Do they listen? Do they ask questions? Do they come back to the conversation and make room for your side?
sections.final_line: What matters isn't one response. It's whether honesty gets easier between you over time.

action_plan.summary: You don't need to understand them more. You need your own feelings to reach the relationship a little sooner.
action_items: Say one small thing on the day it happens instead of carrying it alone.
Use one clear sentence: "That bothered me," "I expected something different," or "I want to talk about this."
Notice whether honest conversations become easier and more mutual over time.

closing.message: You've spent a long time showing love through action. This report looked at why that love could be missed, and how your feelings become clearer when the words arrive sooner.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
